red_baseline.reason: T015 froze canonical clean planning, digest drift, lock/pre-effect ordering, path/evidence retention, journal recovery, and CLI compatibility.
red_baseline.summary: Risky/confirmation exclusions pass; CLI planDigest/mapping cases and Kernel clean handler cases fail only on T016-owned implementation gaps.

validation.summary: 10/10 canonical plan, positive apply, drift, unsafe target, fault/recovery, schema, real CLI, and mapping contracts passed.

validation.summary: 29/29 existing clean and agent-surface baseline tests passed after filtering protected evidence to project-contained paths.

review.evidence: clean.plan and clean.applySafe share one canonical Kernel handler and existing runtime clean authority; apply persists prepared, acquires canonical locks, recomputes current project-contained evidence, refuses digest/path drift before running/deletion, then records one effect; CLI dry-run/safe retain schemaVersion 1 fields while risky all/yes stays CLI-only.

obligation_evidence.evidence: The Kernel clean handler delegates planning, evidence retention, and deletion to the existing runtime/config/run/event/state authorities; no second filesystem or state backend exists.
Normal CLI JSON preserves dryRun/mode/targets/removed and adds planDigest/data.agentResult; text and risky CLI behavior stay stable, and compact remains free of Agent protocol objects.
clean.applySafe is journaled before effects; crash recovery reconciles the original operation and never invokes deletion a second time.
Public mapping exposes only clean.plan and digest-bound clean.applySafe; clean --all --yes is explicitly CLI-only and carries no Agent result.
Application Kernel preflight recomputes the canonical plan while locks are held and transitions prepared directly to refused with plan_digest_mismatch and zero deletion on drift.
The digest binds project/config/safe targets/refusals/protected evidence only; it cannot select risky targets or satisfy the separate human confirmation required by CLI-only clean --all.
Closed public schemas continue rejecting yes, confirmed, approvalToken, riskyTargets, path, and equivalent nested fields before journal/effects.
Fault probes prove prepared precedes revalidation, current plan is checked before running, running precedes the first deletion effect, and recovery evidence remains durable.
Real CLI preview/safe calls and Kernel contracts agree on plan/outcome/resource/effects while retaining permitted schemaVersion 1 formatting and provenance differences.

recovery.action: Restricted protected evidence in the canonical project clean plan to paths contained by the current project root, preventing the newly prepared external journal directory from manufacturing its own digest drift; reran the original T015 suite and existing clean regressions.
